title: Colin Gray found guilty in son's alleged deadly school shooting
fullText: Now PlayingUP NEXT'I never thought I'd be a children's author': Tish Rabe on carrying on Dr. Seuss’ legacy06:13Iran reports 168 dead in strike on elementary school01:53Can Iran survive a war with the United States?03:44Austin attack investigated as possible terrorism01:11House Oversight Committee releases videos of Clinton depositions on Jeffrey Epstein09:37Police release names of victims in Austin bar shooting02:08'Whatever it takes': Trump says the Iran conflict will continue05:10'We'll go as far as we need': Hegseth won't say whether ground troops would be involved in Iran01:13Catherine O'Hara wins posthumous SAG-AFTRA Actor Award for 'The Studio'01:212 people killed and 14 injured in mass shooting in Austin01:58Investigators trying to determine motivation behind shooting at Austin bar01:46First interview with Renee Good’s family03:02Renee Good’s family has yet to watch video of her being shot01:16'I was very clear on the rules': Colin Gray testifies in his own defense 03:13Missing Florida man found alive buried in ‘quick sand’00:30New app is using AI for matchmaking to help users find love04:13Burger King's new AI chatbot to help employees with operations, improve ‘friendliness’02:56Columbia University student released from ICE detention with help of Mamdani 03:40Anthropic CEO says company cannot agree to Pentagon's AI usage demands04:15Now PlayingColin Gray found guilty in son's alleged deadly school shooting03:18UP NEXT'I never thought I'd be a children's author': Tish Rabe on carrying on Dr. Seuss’ legacy06:13Iran reports 168 dead in strike on elementary school01:53Can Iran survive a war with the United States?03:44Austin attack investigated as possible terrorism01:11House Oversight Committee releases videos of Clinton depositions on Jeffrey Epstein09:37
summary: A Georgia jury held Colin Gray criminally responsible for the actions of his teenage son, who fatally shot four people in 2024 at Apalachee High School in Winder. NBC News' Aaron Gilchrist reports on why Gray's verdict is notable.
